channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: The Growing Impact of AI on Human Decision-Making and Critical Thinking
item.description: The concept of 'cognitive surrender,' originally used by theologian Peter L. Berger to describe compromises in faith within pluralistic societies, has been repurposed to describe the modern human reli...

item.title: Solène Lory’s Memorable Stage Moment with Michael Jackson in Munich, 1997
item.description: Solène Lory, a devoted French fan of Michael Jackson, experienced a defining moment of her life during the HIStory World Tour. At 18, she was brought on stage during the Munich concert on July 6, 1997...

item.title: Texas GOP Leaders Disagree on Gas Tax Suspension Following Trump's Proposal
item.description: President Donald Trump's proposal to temporarily suspend the federal gas tax, in response to rising fuel prices influenced by the war in Iran and the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, has created tensi...

item.title: Kristi Noem vacates Coast Guard residence previously occupied by four-star admiral
item.description: Former Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem has finally left a waterfront Coast Guard residence that previously housed Adm. Linda Fagan, the first woman to lead the U.S. Coast Guard. Fagan was repo...

item.title: Body of missing university student discovered as police arrest foreign suspect
item.description: A missing university student has been found dead in a case that has shocked the local community and raised serious safety concerns. According to reports, the student had been reported missing several ...

item.title: David Tlale showcases autumn-winter collection on Gautrain in Joburg
item.description: On a chilly Friday night just after 11pm, renowned South African fashion designer David Tlale brought a unique twist to Joburg’s fashion scene by unveiling his autumn-winter 2026/27 collection on the ...

item.title: Raquel Torres: Pioneering Latina Star of Early Hollywood
item.description: Raquel Torres (1908–1987) was a Mexican-American actress who became one of the most memorable faces of late silent films and early talkies in Hollywood. She rose to fame at just 19 years old with her ...

item.title: VP Sara Duterte says ICC arrest warrant against Bato Dela Rosa cannot be enforced in PH
item.description: Vice President Sara Duterte has publicly dismissed calls for Senator Ronald 'Bato' Dela Rosa to surrender following the International Criminal Court (ICC) arrest warrant issued against him. Speaking f...

item.title: Senate clash over rising terrorism in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa as JUI-F raises concerns
item.description: In the Senate session on Tuesday, treasury benches and opposition members had a heated exchange regarding the increasing incidents of terrorism in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa. JUI-F Senator Attaur Rehman expre...

item.title: Indian Rupee Declines to 95.6 Against US Dollar Amid Rising Oil Prices and Market Volatility
item.description: The Indian financial markets faced significant turbulence on Tuesday as the Sensex fell over 1,450 points, marking a decline of 1.9%, and the Nifty dropped more than 430 points, or 1.8%. Investors suf...

item.title: UK to receive additional cruise passengers for precautionary hantavirus isolation
item.description: Ten passengers from the UK Overseas Territories of Saint Helena and Ascension Island, who were aboard the MV Hondius, are being flown to the UK for precautionary monitoring following a hantavirus outb...

item.title: Behind-the-Scenes Portraits of Audrey Hepburn Filming War and Peace (1956)
item.description: In 1956, Audrey Hepburn took on the iconic role of Natasha Rostova in the epic adaptation of Tolstoy’s 'War and Peace,' directed by King Vidor. These 40 vintage portraits showcase Hepburn both in char...

item.title: Ukraine Begins Preparations for Military Drone Collaboration with Canada
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy has announced that Ukraine is initiating preparations for a military drone agreement with Canada. According to Zelenskyy, 20 countries are already involved in s...

item.title: Victoria Police respond to 161 family violence cases in a single week
item.description: In a single week, Victoria Police’s family violence division in Dandenong dealt with 161 cases, making 95 arrests and recording nearly 300 offences, including assaults, rapes, harassment, and threats ...

item.title: The Future of News: How Established Media Must Adapt to Independent Journalism
item.description: Deborah Turness highlights a profound transformation in the news industry, driven by the shift from traditional broadcast media to independent, personality-led journalism on platforms like YouTube, po...

item.title: Analysis of Jim Chalmers’ 2026 Budget and Its Implications
item.description: The 2026 Budget, presented by Treasurer Jim Chalmers, has been widely criticised for failing to deliver on key pre-election promises. While the Treasurer framed it as a budget aimed at first homebuyer...

item.title: Durban Hosts Africa Travel Indaba 2026 Highlighting Tourism Growth Across the Continent
item.description: Durban is lively as it hosts the Africa Travel Indaba 2026, officially opened by President Cyril Ramaphosa on 12 May at the Inkosi Albert Luthuli Convention Centre in KwaZulu-Natal. The event began wi...

item.title: Tanzania Learns from Vietnam to Improve Nutrition and Women’s Empowerment
item.description: In Tanzania, malnutrition still affects many rural families, especially children and women. The problem is complex, with undernutrition, vitamin deficiencies, and increasing cases of overweight and ob...

item.title: Investigation reveals identity of leading cross-Channel people smuggler
item.description: A BBC investigation has identified a leading people smuggler, previously known only as Kardo Ranya, who is believed to control the majority of illegal cross-Channel journeys to the UK. The 28-year-old...

item.title: ADF paratrooper dies during training exercise in NSW
item.description: Warrant Officer Class Two Lachlan Muddle, a 50-year-old Special Air Service Regiment soldier, tragically died during a parachute training exercise at Jervis Bay Airfield on the New South Wales south c...
